author: @aleethanone6904
text: its not bothersome the idea of supporting you and you are supporting us with this information. but it is bothersome with the membership, that it feels like i am forced to support you in order to get that information, especially because what you are doing feels very meaningful to creating a healthy planet. on that thought i have been wondering myself about what it means to charge people for the things i make them and it only seemed natural at first that i should get paid for making something and giving it to someone. but i am trying to push myself this year to be generous and give away the things i make without charge (i am a woodworker). i am thinking the idea of needing money in return is one of scarcity. that i am afraid i wont have what i need if i don't charge people.  but i have noticed that scarcity is an advertising tool that i have been exposed to a great deal growing up in America and yet when i look around there is actually plenty for everyone and i have always been supported and had what i need. also i believe the universe is going to support me if i am supporting it. in the end i think everyone should do what they are lead to do. i just want to share the idea with you that setting these rules of how you will contribute and what you will get in return, i think, may be detrimental to you and limit you from seeing how the universe is giving back to you for what you are doing. love and peace to you

author: @travismoore7849
text:
Take a large inductance transformer coil with 300mh  and 30mh. Get a 
second one just like it. Now hook a piezo element across the the ground 
and a high antenna wire. Connect one 300mh  coil across the piezo. You 
may connected piezos in series to get higher voltage. Then connect the 
30mh coil to each other. Then measure the output across the free 300mh 
coil for free energy.

replies.author: @WIZ56575
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering we wanted to confirm if the capacitor was charging then discharging and then charging again, we noticed that the meter was fluctuating back and forth maybe for another reason we didn't know but we're trying to understand about how something that stores electrical votes can discharge in charge at the same time or does it have to have some type of switch in it to do so

replies.author: @DryLog420
replies.text: @WIZ56575 The multimeter was disconnected. It was just the normal effect of a disconnected meter jumping around in the 10's of millivolts. Notice when he disconnected  @ 1:03 and the meter dropped, then @ 1:11 he connected the motor. He also mentions that he disconnected the meter.

author: @JazAero
text: Robert I have been watching your channel now for a very many years and I never thought I'd see the day when you sold out. I listened to you countless times talk about how information and ideas should be shared and disseminated and how we should try the things that you put forth in your videos. In doing that I have also had to endure countless advertisements spewed forth from YouTube and from which you derived income from. so for you to now all of a sudden go to a subscription for premium content is not only hypocritical by your own words but just downright rude to your longtime supporters and yes while we may not send you a check every month we still support you through the endless advertisements that are attached to your videos.  What you are doing now is turning your videos into click bait.  This last one specifically so, where you gave out 95% of the information publicly and then leave out that 5% of critical information reserved only for subscribers that makes the whole idea work.  I am very very disappointed in you,  I was hoping to see an energy revolution led by you and instead what were getting is just more mundane and generic "how to" content.

author: @danielade9694
text: Would a jewel thief circuit give a higher output in voltage

replies.author: @OverlandOne
replies.text: Yes, it would.  I was able to boost my output of my earth battery using a variation on the J.T. circuit.  I was then able to light a 48" floro tube, 100 leds brightly (400 leds but not so bright) and run a Bedini style motor.  (Not all at the same time of course)  The J.T. circuit I used had an output of about 350 volts from my earth battery but the key is that is is being pulsed at a high frequency and all of the devices I ran from it responded well to that.  Robert's work here on his channel has surpassed anything I have done on mine.  I have learned a lot from him.

author: @Baigle1
text:
Do you have like a wiki that teaches about why all this works and organizes it for easier study?

Also, matching the antenna size to get the maximum voltage differential per wavelength, as well as making it a dipole quadrupole or hexapole will increase the number of phases that can be picked up. You can even use the quad and hexapole to act as a directional antenna to pick up where the strongest tuned signal is coming from.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: At this stage does it really matter  what the source is? What we are seeing is the harvesting of electrosmog to perform useful work from something that would otherwise just go to waste - later you might well be interested in the sources but the first object has to be demonstrate the idea, then refine the implementation then optimise  - at least that's the way i see it

replies.author: @UnifiedInfo
replies.text: Atmospheric radiation its endless we arent depleting it. It was one Tesla aimed to harness althrough not until recently has it become possible to improve upon. With the precise and effiecent caps and circuitry we have today we could get some usable power. We'd need a big ground buried deep with a surface area equal to or preferably larger then the antennas which would be more effiecent  in  airspace which we cant do sadly lol. But a big metal framed tower with an antenna on it would probably do. I have near enough copper and scrap carbon to weld on but no suffiecent tower.

replies.author: @tristansb979
replies.text: @DiyEcoProjects Make sure to use a very sharp metal tip at the top of the antenna to facilitate electron field emission and drive current more efficiently.

replies.author: @DiyEcoProjects
replies.text:
@tristansb979 Thank you for this. I was planning on just connecting a bit of copper wire to a antenna support cable and see what happens lol. Im guessing that the support cable is insulated from the tall comms tower and so i wont be effecting anything. Hopefully wouldnt get into trouble for that.

Couple of things i wanted to try. Copper wire wrapped around support cable, the circuit, the middle of the circuit to ground attached to a copper square plate pushed in vertically into the soil to ground it. Couple of _ + leads off the circuit and see what we get. Might try a bit of carbon as well

The other thing is i have a DAB radio dongle "receiver only", and wanted to see how many more frequencies i could scan for. Theres some frequencies that are actually pulsars! perhaps I can get france or something. The dongle scans from 0.001khz-9999ghz using SDR# software. 

All the best

author: @aga5897
text:
In principle it's just collecting electromagnetic radiation on many frequencies.
Could be picking up from your mains supply, mobile phone transmitters, TV masts etc.
the Ge/Si diode thing bothers me. Odd you saw no difference - the forward bias voltages are quite different.
It could be the case that neither type of diode 'picks up' on lower intensity EM signals.
Hmm. A system that picks up All E.M radiated energy with no diodes would be a Big thing.
Maybe you're the guy who invents the AC battery. Time will tell.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: now that is a very interesting idea mate

author: @ianfisher6561
text: You could probably build a crystal radio and use the tuned circuit to charge the capacitor. After all this little circuit was used to drive old  inefficient headphones.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it's basically the same circuit mate

replies.author: @ianfisher6561
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Yes I agree, it is just missing the coil and tuning capacitor. Tuning into an AM radio station may give you a bit more power, then rectifying that signal to charge the capacitor. I will probably try that as my next project when I complete the solid state Tesla Coil.

author: @boots7859
text: Would be interesting to see if a faraday cage built around it could modify power input. Preferably different size spacings of 'wire cloth' could be used iteratively to find the primary frequency which is imparting the most energy. After that is found, I would suspect a wavelength specific antenna could be found that might work even better. Even made from the same material.

author: @yougeo
text:
Did you notice the Tshirt design I made for you? maybe it was too many comments ago to see in your scroll. I think it would make you some money if you uploaded it to your teespring website link https://drive.google.com/file/d/1PI1SwT7Aktbrx7TqczznYKm-PFmVTw2A/view?usp=sharing
about this cool circuit: The person suggested using copper "like Tesla" was incorrect because you are harvesting stray electromagnetic signals from the air and not radiant energy like Tesla did and they are different things entirely. Oddly enough teslas circuit to collect radiant energy is even easier to make. frankly a cinch and you should do a comparison.  https://teslauniverse.com/nikola-tesla/patents/us-patent-685957-apparatus-utilization-radiant-energy  What you are collecting is rectified electromagnetic waves from some sources around you like radio frequencies or tv broadcasters signals or something. It wouldnt work in a world with no human made signals (hard to find anywhere on earth though now although I suspect your device wouldnt work in a deep forest far from cities and phone towers) . However the Tesla Radiant energy receiver works from natural sources like the sun. also the tesla collector is directional and the flat plat needs to be pointed in the direction of the strongest source. an ambient tuner like you have built would be less critical with direction unless it was harvesting from one single powerline in your house which was oriented in a certain direction like going up a wall.


Can you please put your oscilloscope on your tuned circuit and see what frequency it is tuned to? It may be able to tune very very broad frequency ranges which may really help its harvesting.  thank you! 



A very cool circuit. very good for people in RV's in the city needing to charge stuff.

replies.text:
@catinvestigation7899 As the mass of polarized light travels through the air, an antenna will be electrically charged depending on the overall voltage of the wave that is bouncing off of it or passing through it. With antennas, you have to adjust the electrical size to match the wavelength, as all radio visible uv xrays etc will move at the speed of light. Its no different to using the kinetic energy in ocean waves to generate power by absorbing part of of the crest and leveling it out with the trough, just using polarized photons which induces a magnetic field and flow of current in conductive material.

Its also unhealthy, increases DNA methylation, and sensitizes the human body to various diseases and cognitive disabilities. Studies on US Embassy workers were quite clear all the way back in the 50s to the early 70s. There are hundreds of well-conducted studies showing similar results in many fields of biomedical science today.

author: @satibel
text: can you cascade them (i.e. put them in series) to get more voltage/power?

replies.author: @Barskor1
replies.text: More than likely.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yep - I would think so

replies.author: @vylbird8014
replies.text: Yes, but with a catch: More stages in the rectifier (it's basically a charge pump) would give you more voltage, but less current. Same power. If you want more power, you need to add more antennas. The equipment quickly gets impractically large.
